nference Marten Lohstroh , Edward A. Lee Citation Marten Lohstroh, Edward A. Lee. "Backward Type Inference". Talk or presentation, 7, November, 2013; Presented at the 10th Biennial Ptolemy Miniconference . Abstract Backward type inference addresses

ence . Abstract Backward type inference addresses the problem of inferring type specifications for dynamic data in Ptolemy II by leveraging its existing type inference mechanism. Ptolemy II is statically typed and therefore requires all ports to reso

lly typed and therefore requires all ports to resolve to a type prior execution. When an actor mediates access to untyped data, or data is subject to unexpected changes, there is no way to reliably infer the type of its output from the actor itself.

fer the type of its output from the actor itself. Manual annotations are tedious and make models more brittle than necessary. The basic idea is to let type constraints imposed by downstream actors determine the type of the otherwise underdetermine

determine the type of the otherwise underdetermined output ports of actors that mediate access to untyped resources. This is achieved using additional type constraints between and within actors. Backward inferred types serve the goal of achieving max

ard inferred types serve the goal of achieving maximally permissive composition, as they are specific enough not to limit composability and general enough not to impose unnecessary constraints on the data. This solution has been implemented in Pto

data. This solution has been implemented in Ptolemy II and the goal of this poster is to familiarize users with its function. Electronic downloads BackwardTypeInference.pdf · application/pdf · 3750 kbytes Citation formats HTML Marten Lohstroh, Edw
